title: Bill Reichenbach Jr.

infobox.value: William Frank Reichenbach Jr.   (1949-11-30) November 30, 1949 (age 76)  [Takoma Park, Maryland](/wiki/Takoma_Park,_Maryland "Takoma Park, Maryland"), U.S.

[Bass trombone](/wiki/Bass_trombone "Bass trombone") [Tenor trombone](/wiki/Tenor_trombone "Tenor trombone") [Contrabass trombone](/wiki/Contrabass_trombone "Contrabass trombone") [Euphonium](/wiki/Euphonium "Euphonium") [Tuba](/wiki/Tuba "Tuba")

(born November 30, 1949) is an American jazz trombonist and composer. He is the son of [Bill Reichenbach](/wiki/William_Frank_Reichenbach_Sr. "William Frank Reichenbach Sr."), who was the drummer for [Charlie Byrd](/wiki/Charlie_Byrd "Charlie Byrd") from 1962 to 1973. He is best known as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") for television, films, cartoons, and commercials.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He primarily specializes in playing the [bass trombone](/wiki/Bass_trombone "Bass trombone"), however, he is also proficient in playing other instruments such as the [tenor trombone](/wiki/Tenor_trombone "Tenor trombone"), [contrabass trombone](/wiki/Contrabass_trombone "Contrabass trombone"), [euphonium](/wiki/Euphonium "Euphonium"), and [tuba](/wiki/Tuba "Tuba").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

first_paragraph: **William Frank Reichenbach Jr.** (born November 30, 1949) is an American jazz trombonist and composer. He is the son of [Bill Reichenbach](/wiki/William_Frank_Reichenbach_Sr. "William Frank Reichenbach Sr."), who was the drummer for [Charlie Byrd](/wiki/Charlie_Byrd "Charlie Byrd") from 1962 to 1973. He is best known as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") for television, films, cartoons, and commercials.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He primarily specializes in playing the [bass trombone](/wiki/Bass_trombone "Bass trombone"), however, he is also proficient in playing other instruments such as the [tenor trombone](/wiki/Tenor_trombone "Tenor trombone"), [contrabass trombone](/wiki/Contrabass_trombone "Contrabass trombone"), [euphonium](/wiki/Euphonium "Euphonium"), and [tuba](/wiki/Tuba "Tuba").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Musical artist

**William Frank Reichenbach Jr.** (born November 30, 1949) is an American jazz trombonist and composer. He is the son of [Bill Reichenbach](/wiki/William_Frank_Reichenbach_Sr. "William Frank Reichenbach Sr."), who was the drummer for [Charlie Byrd](/wiki/Charlie_Byrd "Charlie Byrd") from 1962 to 1973. He is best known as a [session musician](/wiki/Session_musician "Session musician") for television, films, cartoons, and commercials.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He primarily specializes in playing the [bass trombone](/wiki/Bass_trombone "Bass trombone"), however, he is also proficient in playing other instruments such as the [tenor trombone](/wiki/Tenor_trombone "Tenor trombone"), [contrabass trombone](/wiki/Contrabass_trombone "Contrabass trombone"), [euphonium](/wiki/Euphonium "Euphonium"), and [tuba](/wiki/Tuba "Tuba").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 1997 he recorded a solo album, *Special Edition*, where he is featured on tenor as well as bass trombone.

## Early life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bill_Reichenbach_Jr.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career")]

Reichenbach Jr. was born and raised in [Takoma Park](/wiki/Takoma_Park,_Maryland "Takoma Park, Maryland"), [Maryland](/wiki/Maryland "Maryland"). He began playing in high school for bands in the Washington, D.C. area, and also sat in with his father's group, where he played with [Milt Jackson](/wiki/Milt_Jackson "Milt Jackson"), [Zoot Sims](/wiki/Zoot_Sims "Zoot Sims"), and others.

He went to [Rochester, New York](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") to study at the [Eastman School of Music](/wiki/Eastman_School_of_Music "Eastman School of Music") with the legendary teacher [Emory Remington](/wiki/Emory_Remington "Emory Remington"), and after graduating, joined the [Buddy Rich](/wiki/Buddy_Rich "Buddy Rich") band.

In 1975, he decided to relocate to [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), where he would play with the [Toshiko Akiyoshi – Lew Tabackin Big Band](/wiki/Toshiko_Akiyoshi_%E2%80%93_Lew_Tabackin_Big_Band "Toshiko Akiyoshi – Lew Tabackin Big Band") and the Don Menza Big Band and Sextet in the mid/late 1970s. After his move, he became known for music for television and film.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

He played trombone on *[The Wiz](/wiki/The_Wiz_(film) "The Wiz (film)")* and, with the [Seawind Horns](/wiki/Seawind_Horns "Seawind Horns") including [Jerry Hey](/wiki/Jerry_Hey "Jerry Hey"), on [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson")'s albums *[Off the Wall](/wiki/Off_the_Wall_(album) "Off the Wall (album)")* and *[Thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(Michael_Jackson_album) "Thriller (Michael Jackson album)")*. He also acted as a composer for *[Cartoon All-Stars to the Rescue](/wiki/Cartoon_All-Stars_to_the_Rescue "Cartoon All-Stars to the Rescue").*

Bill Reichenbach is a Greenhoe Trombones Performing Artist,[[5]](#cite_note-ref1-5) performing on a GC5-2R-TIS Bass Trombone with Tuning-in-Slide.[[6]](#cite_note-ref3-6)
